senses.gloss: to emerge; to come up; to spring; to issue; to rise

examples.text: يَوْمَئِذٍ يَصْدُرُ النَّاسُ أَشْتَاتًا لِّيُرَوْا أَعْمَالَهُمْ
examples.english: On that Day people will proceed in separate groups to be shown [the consequences of] their deeds.

senses.gloss: to emerge; to come up; to spring; to issue; to rise; to materialize, to happen, to occur
to emerge; to come up; to spring; to issue; to rise; to emerge as if from a source, to proceed, to emanate, to arise, to originate, to stem, to spring
to emerge; to come up; to spring; to issue; to rise; to be released; to be public or available, to be out; to come out; to circulate

examples.text: صَدَرَ الْكِتَابُ مِنْ دَارِ النَّشْرِ يَوْمَ الْأَرْبِعَاء.
examples.english: The book issued from the publisher on Wednesday.

senses.gloss: to send, to send off, to send out; to export
to send, to send off, to send out; to dispatch; to forward; to transport
to send, to send off, to send out; to publish, to issue, to promulgate
verbal noun of صَدَرَ (ṣadara, “to emerge; to materialize; to be released publicly”) (form I)
verbal noun of صَدَرَ (ṣadara, “to hit in the chest”) (form I)
a chest, a breast; a bust, a bosom
a chest, a breast; something resembling, likened to, or related to chests (such as a center, a core, a heart, and so on)
a front; a person at the front, a leader, a commander
a beginning, a start, an outset, a commencement; an inception; a dawn, a rise
to hurt the chest of; to hit in the chest
